text:
In a breathtaking photo finish, US megastar Noah Lyles has cemented his place as the fastest man in the world after winning the 100m sprint at the 2024 Paris Olympics.

The Paris 2024 Olympic Games has crowned a new fastest man in the world as United States sprint star Noah Lyles clinched gold in the 100m sprint on Sunday.

The US sprinter penned his name alongside former gold medallists Usain Bolt, Carl Lewis and Jesse Owens as he scraped past the competition to win by the slimmest of margins – 0.005 seconds.

The photo finish was so close that the runners were forced to wait on the track and stare at the stadium’s big screen to see the result.

After a drawn-out review, officials handed Lyles the gold medal with a time of 9.784 seconds.

To the roar of almost 80,000 spectators at the Stade de France, Lyles, draped in the stars and stripes, roared to cameras: “America, I told you, I got next!”

In an interview with Nine’s Tony Jones, Lyles said: “There was a lot of doubt, I am not going to lie.”

“I thought I didn’t outdo Kishane there, I thought he outdid me. When I crossed the line, I went up to him and said, ‘Hey, man, I think you got that one’.

“When it came out, I thought, ‘Oh my gosh, I am amazing’.”

The 27-year-old beat out rival Jamaican Kishane Thompson (9.789) and fellow American Fred Kerley (9.81).

The race was the first time in Olympic history all 100m runners had qualified for the final in under 10 seconds.

Lyles’ path to victory had been anything but easy as the Florida-born sprinter bypassed the traditional US college athlete route to turn professional.

The American had spent the leadup to the Olympics working on his technique and practicing his infamously poor start with coach Lance Brauman.

When the starting gun fired, his 0.178 reaction time set him off the blocks in last place as he torched his way down the straight.

His speed saved him as he gradually fought his way back among the pack by 50m and threw himself over the line to overtake Thompson by a hair’s breadth.

While Thompson actually broke the line first with his foot, it was the American whose chest first passed the finishing line – the body part which determines the victor.

The victory makes Lyles the first United States Olympic champion in 20 years – since Justin Gatlin won at Athens in 2004.

Lyles has set himself the goal of winning gold in the 100m, 200m and 4x100m relay after achieving the trifecta at the World Championship in Budapest.

The outspoken sprinter will now turn his focus onto the 200m sprint event which will begin on Monday before the final on Thursday (local time).

title: United States sprint star Noah Lyles seizes Olympic Gold in photo finish 100m Sprint at Paris 2024 | Sky News Australia

keywords: olympic games,paris,stade de france,100 sprint,100m,fastest man in the world,paris 2024,100m final,noah lyles
